[@microsoft/fast-element](../fast-element/index.html) &gt; [NodeBehaviorOptions](../fast-element.nodebehavioroptions/index.html) &gt; [single](../fast-element.nodebehavioroptions.single/index.html)

## NodeBehaviorOptions.single property

Assigns a single node to the property instead of an array of nodes.

**Signature:**

```typescript
single?: boolean;
```

## Remarks

The node assigned is the first match in document order after `filter` is applied; `single` does not assert that only one node matches. When nothing matches, `null` is assigned.

Because node observation includes text nodes by default, pair this with `filter: elements()` or the whitespace text node between tags is usually what gets assigned.

When combined with `flatten: true` on `slotted`<!-- -->, an empty slot resolves to the slot's default content, so the first default content node is assigned rather than `null`<!-- -->.

Do not pre-initialize the target property to `null`<!-- -->. Observables only notify on a change, so a property already holding `null` that binds against an empty slot or an empty child list will not run its change callback.
